reviews.review_msg: Daria is one of the nicest people I have ever met :) she truly cares about the well being of her students, and wants you to succeed. Participation is important, but she doesn't make you feel afraid to speak! For sure would take a course from her again.

Daria is such a great Italian Professor. She is so caring and nice and she is a true Italian so you really get taught authentically. The tests and quizzes were super fair. The final was pretty easy and it was a low maintenance course because there are no readings and you can cram before because it is basically all just memorization.

Daria is the best, most caring, most understanding prof I have ever had in my entire life (or 4 years at SFU). She goes above and beyond for her students, and is extremely understanding if in need of an extension. She genuinely wants you to "find your italian side" and is so passionate about what she teaches. Sincerely is a gem of a person!

Daria is the best teacher i've ever had at SFU. Her Italian class is fun, energetic, interesting and easy to follow. She is hilarious so she makes everything super fun and easy to learn but also makes the class that much better!! Would take her for every single class if i could! Highly reccommend:)
ADORABLE! best teacher I have ever had in SFU. I highly recommend.

She is absolutely wonderful! Genuinely cares about students and is extremely passionate about the course. A lot of activities are required, but that helps to learn the language. Make sure to go to class and participate in order to get an A. Access code purchased with the textbook is useless- don't buy. Overall, best Italian prof I have ever had!
Daria is so nice and approachable. Just don't hesitate to ask her any questions if you need help. But make sure to read the textbook before class, that's nothing to worry about.

Daria is by far one of the best professors at SFU. She is very organized, flexible and genuinely cares about her students. She is passionate and is always there if you need extra help. The material was interesting and her exams are fair. If you put in the effort, you will do well. I highly recommend doing this course with her.

Daria is a wonderful prof!  She is so sweet and always willing to help you.  Very approachable, a fair marker, and super helpful when it comes to assignmentscourse work.  She told us about Italian culture and often lightened up harder classes with a funny video or a song.  I'm taking the next level of Italian because of her!

Amazing prof. Keeps the class interesting, is structured and clear, has an agenda, organized. Tolerates an appropriate amount of fooling around/talking/inattentiveness yet will politely bring your attention back if she feels necessary. Very chill-- easy to talk to, you can joke with her. Great prof, tests are normal difficulty but fair :)

She is a great teacher, with a great laugh, and a presence that fills the room. She is keen to sticking with the textbook, and will set you towards the right path in the course without being strict, negative, or harsh. A pleasure to learn from! Funny, and she has an AMAZING smile that brightens up the class.

Believe don't take her class! Take other ITAL teacher's class! The mark she give is really unfair,this is 100 level class not 200 or 300 level but she always marks like it's a high level class. If you want get a fair and higher mark avoid this teacher!!!By the way, she's class is so boring and under pressure!
Her class is a bit dry. other than that she is a great teacher.

One of the best profs at SFU. She's adorable and cares lots about all of her students. Class average was an A. You will have to review the materials before and after class because it is very fast paced, but she gives lots of worksheets and materials for review. I would recommend this course to anyone regardless if you have an italian background.
She is the loveliest prof ever. I absolutely recommend taking Italian courses with her.

Very nice professor. There are so much things to cover in this class, and she tried and did manage to teach material pretty well in only couple months. She is super helpful when you visit during her office hours.  You do need to study and review material every day in order to succeed in this course.. Grammar part is little tricky, but manageable.
Chohre is definitely one of my favourite professors at SFU! She's very helpful and responds to emails. Very fair assignments and exams.

Took this as an intersession course. Definitely need to show up to class. But it was great. She's adorable and really cares about the students. She won't take marks off quizzes if everyone chose something that she thinks is obvious in an Italian point of view.   Best class I've taken in my SFU career.

She is a very helpful teacher, very passionate of what she is doing. Sometimes she plays an Italian song and funny video to wake the class up. However, there are a lot of online assignment to keep you going. Be prepared to review the text everyday! I have taken both Japanese and Italian at SFU.i like ITAL more since she is teaching it:)

She's such a good prof - a cute little old Italian lady! She's kind of funny and loves to talk about Italy. Sometimes she's unclear, but it's because things that are really obvious to her in Italian might be confusing to us. Fast paced. Fun. 100% textbook use. All online assignments. Available for help. Understanding. Loved this course!

In this class, we had to cover 7 chapters in a few 4 months. The pace was EXTREMELY fast. To do well, you must review the material everyday. If you do not do this, you will be lost. This is not a class I would recommend to someone without any prior background in Italian. Be prepared to dedicate a lot of your time to this class.

A very nice woman.  She uses the text for everything.  Sometimes a little harsh when you make a beginners mistake in class.  Encourages a lot of participation but it is a language course.. so expected.
LOVE this teacher. She is so nice and helpful. I'm planning to take the rest of the Italian courses offered, i hope she is teaching them.

She is the best teacher anyone can ask for:)  She is clear in her teaching, she can be approached and you'll be treated with kindness and consideration!  She has a clear passion about her teachiing and her students, and about Italy... obviously one of the reasons why she is absolutely the best teacher.

Very nice and understanding. Always willing to help students understand. Passionate about Italy and the language, and helpful not only in teaching the language, but teaching the culture of Italy. I would recommend this class to everyone.
very very nice teacher. very helpful and understanding.
Such a sweet teacher - not intimidating whatsoever, very helpful and understanding. Fun class!

Totally unclear w the criteria and loves to pick you on whenever you clearly have no idea what the answer is. Class went south thru out time. Nothing is worth doing bc it requires strenuous effort yet only worth 10% each.. leaves you feeling humiliated during and after class despite putting effort w the pre knowledge.

I am currently taking ITAL 100 with Professor Vintila and he is one of the best teachers I've had in my life. He truly made me appreciate Italian language at an entirely other level. He is so caring and is always there if you need help. He is always accomodating to his student's needs and engages the whole class in his lectures. 110% recommended.
Such an inspiring professor. Make sure to attend classes to engage and you'll learn very well. A very fun course to take. Italian is not that hard, just need to get used to it.

really terrible. he does not use the course syllabus and I found this out when grades came back. his tests are too long and he takes too long with the listening, like we waste most of the test on it. he should grade on the syllabus that he showed us he needs to reduce his tests length and fix his grading then he would be a 3.5/5.

You teach yourself all the course material from the textbook while Vlad only goes over the questions during lectures. He is not clear on what he's looking for, and gives low grades. Also, he likes to pick on the people who do not understand the language. I left all of the INTRO classes feeling humiliated. BTW, a lot more difficult than it should be

Vlad is a great guy. He is so passionate about the subject and he takes the time to make sure everyone understood what they were doing. If you don't pay attention in class it will be confusing, but that's on you. All tests and quizzes consisted of stuff we went over in class many times. He is very interactive which makes lectures engaging.

Vlad is a great prof. He truly cares for his students' well-being. He even goes out of his way to make students understand course material and feel supported throughout their learning journey. Also, he is very genuine and super knowledgeable! I heard he speaks like five different languages, which makes him even cooler!
